🎧 Stream Signals: Decoding the DNA of Spotify Hits

How audio structure, cluster archetypes, and interaction-based modeling explain track popularity.

ᯤ Spotify Data Story

What Audio Features Makes a Spotify Song Popular?

Observations
🎵 24,739 Songs
Inputs
🧠 15 Audio Features
Target
🔥 Track Popularity

Popularity Rating

Correlation Matrix

Key Takeaways
  • Popularity is concentrated in the middle-to-upper rating buckets, with rating 3 appearing most often.
  • The strongest positive relationship is between Energy and Loudness (r = 0.69), followed by Valence and Danceability (r = 0.34).
  • The strongest negative relationship is between Energy and Acousticness (r = -0.55).
▶︎•၊၊||၊|။||||။၊|။•

Clustering

Clustering was used to segment songs into eight audio archetypes based on their standardized audio feature patterns, showing how Spotify’s catalog separates into distinct listening environments, moods, and use cases before prediction ever begins.

Cluster Size and Mean Rating

Bars show how much of the dataset each cluster captures, while the line shows each segment’s average popularity rating on the 0–5 scale.

Cluster Feature Heatmap

Stream Signals: Spotify's Soundwaves

CLUSTER KEY TAKEAWAYS

⚡ Cluster 0 · Niche High-Energy Tracks · ★ 1.86

↑ Instrumentalness · ↑ Energy · ↓ Acousticness

Vibe: club sets, high-intensity workouts, late-night energy

🎤 Cluster 1 · Live Crowd Movers · ★ 2.38

↑ Liveness · ↑ Energy · ↓ Acousticness

Vibe: concerts, parties, and social spaces

🌙 Cluster 2 · Reflective Energy · ★ 2.45

↑ Energy · ↑ Loudness · ↓ Danceability

Vibe: studying, commuting, or solo listening

🔥 Cluster 3 · Mainstream Hit Core · ★ 2.72

↑ Tempo · ↑ Speechiness · ↓ Instrumentalness

Vibe: radio-ready hits, parties, and mainstream playlists

🕺 Cluster 4 · Dancefloor Hooks · ★ 2.51

↑ Speechiness · ↑ Danceability · ↓ Tempo

Vibe: celebrations, dance-heavy settings, and group energy

🌿 Cluster 5 · Ambient Calm · ★ 2.52

↑ Instrumentalness · ↑ Acousticness · ↓ Energy

Vibe: relaxation, focus, and calm background listening

☀️ Cluster 6 · Upbeat Feel-Good · ★ 2.64

↑ Valence · ↑ Danceability · ↓ Speechiness

Vibe: workouts, upbeat social playlists, and feel-good momentum

☕ Cluster 7 · Acoustic Intimacy · ★ 2.58

↑ Acousticness · ↑ Duration Ms · ↓ Energy

Vibe: coffee shops, acoustic sets, and quiet nights

↻ ◁ || ▷ ↺

Predictive Modeling

Eight logistic models were run to test whether increasingly rich combinations of categorical context, continuous audio features, and interaction terms improve hit prediction. The final story focuses on Model 6 because it is the clear winner across both discrimination and validation.

Modeling Overview
  • Eight logistic models were tested, starting with a baseline and gradually adding categorical effects, continuous audio features, interaction terms, and polynomial structure.
  • The progression is designed to answer whether richer musical relationships improve hit prediction beyond simple additive effects.
  • Model 6 is the focal model because it wins on both accuracy and ROC AUC while holding up under cross-validation.
View full model progression table
Model # Description Accuracy ROC AUC Number of Coefficients

2 Additive categorical inputs 0.622620 0.629985 27
3 Additive continuous inputs 0.625652 0.628944 11
4 Additive categorical + continuous inputs 0.647399 0.678529 27
5 Categorical interactions 0.640850 0.650461 56
6 Interactions: categorical × continuous 0.675128 0.711825 287
7 Interactions + polynomial structure 0.632564 0.635940 21
8 Most flexible extended interaction model 0.657383 0.687880 87
9 NaN 0.618416 0.500000 1
10 NaN 0.622620 0.629985 27
11 NaN 0.625652 0.628944 11

16 NaN 0.657383 0.687880 87
Model Performance

Interactions Model (Categorical and Continuous) · 0.7036
Coefficient Count
287
Model 6 is the richest specification carried into the final story.

ROC Comparison Plot

Model 6 produces the strongest discrimination among the eight candidate specifications.

Why It Matters
  • Model 6 wins on both accuracy and ROC AUC, so the same specification is selected regardless of metric.
  • Its advantage over the intercept-only baseline confirms that audio structure plus musical context contain real predictive signal.
  • The higher coefficient count reflects a richer interaction structure rather than a purely additive view of track behavior.

Testing Accuracy Comparison Plot

Shortlisted models were stress-tested with cross-validation, and Model 6 remained the strongest choice.

Validation Takeaways
  • Model 6 wins on both accuracy and ROC AUC, so the same specification is selected regardless of metric.
  • Its advantage over the intercept-only baseline confirms that audio structure plus musical context contain real predictive signal.
  • The higher coefficient count reflects a richer interaction structure rather than a purely additive view of track behavior.

Model 6 Coefficients

The coefficient view highlights which terms contribute the most positive and negative weight inside the winning interaction model.

Interpretation Takeaways
  • Strong positive effects in the top-magnitude view include C(Decade)[T.1960S]:Speechiness, Playlist Genre[T.R&B], and C(Playlist Genre)[T.Pop]:Speechiness.
  • The strongest negative effects include C(Decade)[T.1970S]:Speechiness, Playlist Genre[T.Rap], and C(Decade)[T.1980S]:Speechiness.
  • The spread around zero shows that Model 6 is learning both lift and drag effects rather than pushing every feature in the same direction.

Applied Prediction · Model 6

Predicted probabilities are plotted across energy and loudness to show how genre-specific interaction effects change the hit landscape.

Prediction Takeaways
  • Across Model 6, higher loudness generally aligns with stronger predicted hit probability.
  • At lower loudness levels, prediction differences by genre are more separated, but those relationships shift as loudness increases.
  • The interaction structure is what makes the model useful: the effect of energy changes depending on genre and loudness rather than staying fixed.
Closing Note

Final Synthesis

Final synthesis. Taken together, the results show that Spotify popularity is not driven by one universal recipe. Songs sort into clear audio archetypes, and the strongest predictive model succeeds because it lets feature effects change with musical context. In this dataset, hit potential is shaped less by isolated features than by how those features interact inside the right kind of song.

Data Source: Tidy Tuesday Spotify Songs Dataset (cleaned CSV).

Methodology: Exploratory Data Analysis → Clustering → Predictive Modeling → exported as a standalone HTML data story.
